title: brassica repanda subsp. cantabrica
intro: Brassica repanda subsp. cantabrica, also known as the Cantabrian or Spanish horseradish, is a plant species within the Brassica genus, characterized by its strong pungent root and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Brassica repanda subsp. cantabrica include its ability to relieve sinus congestion, reduce inflammation, and provide antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's root and leaves are typically used fresh or dried and can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
